title: Reading log — One Hundred Years of Solitude (Chapters 7–20)
html:
TL;DR

Despite the inflammatory title ("Made people vote to prove voting is a farce. only effective thing is violence"), 95% of this note is a chapter-by-chapter reading log of one hundred years of solitude by gabriel garcia marquez, Chapters 7 through 20. The first sentence is either a bookmark from the chapter Yahir was reading (the banana-company massacre arc) or his own provocation — it's never returned to. The rest is patient note-taking on the Buendía family.

Themes Yahir consistently pulls out:

  • cyclical time — Úrsula's "time is going in a circle"; the family repeating itself; days that "just happen."
  • lineage as fate — every Aureliano stamped with ash, repeating their predecessors' obsessions; Melquíades' pre-written history.
  • The banana-company massacre — wages paid in scrip, the train-station machine guns, no proof the next day. ("Authorities unleash machine guns on rowdy crowd, but the next day there is no proof of it happening.")
  • Repressed and forbidden love — Mauricio Babilonia's spinal-cord shooting; Aureliano and Amaranta Úrsula; the pig-tail baby.

The note ends abruptly with two unrelated lines: "Go beyond the surface go" and "Code tenderloin" — the second sounds like a programming reference (CS coursework era).

Key quotes (Yahir's, condensing the novel)

"Time isn't passing its looping in a circle"

"[Aureliano's] lineage is like a wheel of rearing events that would continue on for [eternity] if it weren't for the determination of the axel"

"The kid was born, he had the tail of a pig. Full circle to the worry incest."

"Only one in a centering [century] who had been engendered with love"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The first long-form reading log in the vault — confirms Yahir reads literary fiction patiently and synthesizes themes (not just plot beats).
  • cyclical time is a load-bearing concept node: it links forward to any later notes about generations, AI, the man from the future, and pre-AGI legacy.
  • The violence as only effective tool standalone line is the only "political manifesto" voltage in the note — keep an eye on whether it returns.

Open questions

  • Is there a Chapter 1–6 log somewhere earlier in 2022 raw notes?
  • "Code tenderloin" — CS coursework reference, or a place name (Tenderloin SF)? Worth a grep.
  • Did the "Go beyond the surface go" tag mean to start a new note?

Wikilinks
